agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"database","status":"correct","issue":"Health check includes database dependency verification"},{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"message-broker","status":"correct","issue":"Retry with exponential backoff (max 3) handles transient publish failures"},{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"email-provider","status":"correct","issue":"Circuit breaker with 30s reset protects against downstream email outages"},{"from":"external-callers","to":"payment-notification-service","status":"correct","issue":"Structured logging with request ID enables full request tracing"}],"contract_compliance":"All described integrations follow defensive patterns: retries on broker, circuit breaker on email, timeouts on all outbound HTTP. Contracts appear sound.","data_flow_integrity":"Request flow is traceable end-to-end via request ID in structured logs. Dependency health is verified via /health endpoint."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["Verify that failed message payloads are logged (redacted) for replay/debugging","Confirm circuit breaker state transitions (open/half-open/closed) are logged so on-call knows when email provider is degraded"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Good — request rate, error rate, and p99 latency via Prometheus cover the three core signals","gaps":["Add circuit breaker state metric (0/1 gauge) so dashboards and alerts can fire on provider degradation","Add retry count histogram to detect broker instability before full failures surface","Add message broker publish latency metric to distinguish broker slowness from email provider slowness","Consider adding a metric for notification delivery success vs failure (business-level SLI, not just HTTP-level)"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Request ID present in logs enables correlation. No mention of distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry spans).","gaps":["If this service participates in a multi-service payment flow, propagate trace context (W3C traceparent) so the full payment lifecycle is traceable across services"]},"debugging_capability":"Strong foundation. Structured JSON logs with request ID, duration, status, and error details give an on-call engineer what they need to triage most incidents. The gaps above are improvements, not blockers."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Message broker unavailable","handling":"handled","recommendation":"3 retries with exponential backoff is appropriate. Verify a dead-letter queue or persistent fallback exists for messages that exhaust all retries — otherwise payment notifications are silently lost."},{"scenario":"Email provider down","handling":"handled","recommendation":"Circuit breaker with 30s reset is solid. Confirm that notifications blocked by an open circuit are queued for later delivery, not dropped."},{"scenario":"Database unavailable","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Health check detects it, but confirm the service returns 503 and stops accepting new work rather than failing mid-request. Readiness vs liveness probe distinction matters here."},{"scenario":"Outbound HTTP timeout (5s)","handling":"handled","recommendation":"5s timeout is reasonable. Verify it applies to connection establishment AND read separately — a 5s connect timeout is too generous; consider 2s connect / 5s read."},{"scenario":"Poison message / malformed payload","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No mention of input validation or dead-letter handling for malformed messages. A single poison message could consume all retry attempts repeatedly. Add validation and route bad messages to a DLQ."},{"scenario":"Memory/thread exhaustion under load","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No mention of rate limiting or backpressure. If upstream sends a burst of payment events, the service could exhaust connections to email provider or database. Consider concurrency limits."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff with max 3 retries on broker publish — appropriate. Verify jitter is included to avoid thundering herd on broker recovery.","timeout_config":"5s on all outbound HTTP calls — reasonable. Recommend splitting into connect and read timeouts for finer control.","circuit_breakers":"30s reset on email provider — appropriate for a notification path. Confirm half-open state allows a single probe request before fully closing.","graceful_degradation":"Circuit breaker provides graceful degradation for email. Confirm notifications that cannot be sent are persisted for retry rather than lost — this is a payment-related service and delivery guarantees matter."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":true,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. No schema migrations in this release, so rollback is clean with no data compatibility risk.","monitoring_coverage":"Core HTTP metrics (rate, errors, p99) are present. Add circuit breaker state gauge and retry count histogram for full operational visibility. These are recommended but not blocking.","on_call_documentation":"Ensure a runbook exists covering: (1) what to do when circuit breaker opens on email provider, (2) how to replay failed messages from DLQ, (3) how to verify broker connectivity, (4) how to interpret structured logs for a given request ID.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":["Dead-letter queue for messages that exhaust retries (if not already provisioned)"],"config_changes":[],"infra_provisioning":[]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema migrations in this release. Rollback is a clean container image swap."},"verdict":"READY WITH CAVEATS"},"escalations":[{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"No dead-letter queue or fallback for messages that exhaust broker retries","reason":"This is a payment notification service. Silently dropping notifications after 3 failed retries is a business-critical data loss risk. Must confirm DLQ exists or add one.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["Rollback safety — no migrations, clean image swap","Core observability — structured logging and Prometheus metrics are solid","Resilience patterns — retries, circuit breaker, and timeouts are well-configured"],"low_confidence_areas":["Dead-letter queue existence — not mentioned, critical for payment notifications","Poison message handling — no input validation strategy described","Backpressure/rate limiting under load — not described","Kubernetes probe configuration (readiness vs liveness) — not described"],"assumptions":["Service runs in a container orchestrator with health check integration","Message broker supports dead-letter queue semantics","Circuit breaker implementation follows standard half-open probe pattern","Exponential backoff includes jitter"]}}
